steps.title: Identify Ortega's decisive counterpoint
steps.content: Ortega directly contests the “hyper-realistic” interpretation, arguing instead that the vessels were creative works designed to explore abstract concepts—not literal records of identifiable people's actual features.

steps.title: Apply to the Text 1 interpretation
steps.content:
Ortega contends that these facial markings are artistic devices—not literal records—through which sculptors explored abstract ideas.

The other choices either make irrelevant comparisons, reinforce the “hyper-realistic” reading Ortega opposes, or raise points outside the texts.

That confirms D is correct.
